description_long: This is a three-story commercial pastry shop with a classic European facade, featuring beige stucco walls and green shuttered windows. The ground floor has a large awning in a muted tan color, with the name 'SALIERTS' prominently displayed above the entrance. The upper floors have symmetrically arranged windows with decorative white trim and green shutters, and a small balcony with a black wrought-iron railing is visible above the entrance. The sidewalk in front is paved, and an early 20th-century style car is parked nearby, suggesting a historical or vintage urban setting. The building's overall appearance is well-maintained, with subtle weathering on the lower facade and a flat roof.
description: A three-story beige pastry shop with green shutters, a tan awning labeled 'SALIERTS', and a classic European facade. Features include a small balcony, decorative window trim, and a vintage car parked outside.
description_view_0: Side view showing a plain stucco wall, three shuttered windows, and a partial view of the entrance and vintage car.
description_view_1: Front view with the 'SALIERTS' awning, main entrance, full window arrangement, balcony, and the vintage car parked directly in front.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a plain wall, three shuttered windows, and the car parked to the left; the awning and entrance are not visible.
description_view_3: Rear view with six shuttered windows and a plain, undecorated lower facade; no entrance or signage visible.
attribute_storefront_signage: The storefront features a large, rectangular tan awning with the word 'SALIERTS' in bold, white, serif capital letters centered above the entrance. The signage is simple and classic, with no additional logos or decorative elements, and is positioned directly above the main door, spanning the width of the shopfront.
attribute_architectural_style: The architectural style is reminiscent of late 19th to early 20th-century European urban buildings, with a stucco facade, symmetrical window placement, green louvered shutters, decorative white window trim, and a small wrought-iron balcony. The flat roof and understated ornamentation suggest a blend of Neoclassical and early Modernist influences.
